story-3a4795e0dcbc0fb32a159710.content: Netanyahu Sums Up Israel’s Achievements in Lebanon So Far, and Promises a Solution to the Drones by Hugh Fitzgerald The biggest military challenge to Israel and the Western alliance today comes from drones; they are cheap to manufacture and deliver a powerful blow; they are launched in waves, too numerous for them all to be intercepted. And the intercepting missiles cost far more than the drones themselves. Now Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu promises that Israeli weapons scientists will be the first to come up with an answer to the challenge of drone warfare. The Islamic Republic of Iran is threatening the Trump administration, buoyed the tens of billions of dollars now flowing to it because American sanctions have been lifted, with much more to come from the assets about to be unfrozen. Tehran insists that it will not open the Strait of Hormuz until Israel withdraws entirely from southern Lebanon. Netanyahu has made clear that Israel will remain in its “security zone” in Lebanon unless, and not until, Hezbollah is completely disarmed. And his pledge that Israeli scientists will be the first to find a solution to the problem of explosive drones, that have been used not against Israel, but also against American military bases in the region, is not an idle boast. Given the record of these scientists’ past achievements in military matters, Netanyahu was expressing not a wish, but a promise. Read more at the link in the next post.

story-583b84ffbeeb54416f63d72c.content: Let's be clear about what's been going on in Venezuela so the White House doesn't get away with politicizing these people's suffering for propaganda purposes: Trump removed Maduro from power, but left the oppressive government intact in return for their "cooperation." The regime, in fact, did NOT "change." The people were NOT "liberated." The "oil flowing" narrative is spin. The long term, trillion dollar rebuild Trump is describing is essentially a fantasy at this point. It would require political stability, which Venezuela does not have, and legal frameworks and profitability conditions that don't yet exist. Major oil companies have, at BEST, adopted a "wait-and-see" stance. They're hesitant to commit to such a large scale, irreversible, slapdash, and incomplete operation. The gap between Trump's rhetoric and actual industry behavior is MASSIVE. The CEO of ExxonMobil said so to his face. Nevertheless, Trump still used Venezuela as a launching point for his war in Iran, a war that he LOST by conceding bargaining power to the Iranian regime in practically all matters, leaving them in a stronger strategic position than before the war, and allowing them to harness their control over the Strait of Hormuz as a weapon of leverage. The people of Venezuela have since turned on Trump because he's thrown his support behind the regime and has done nothing to improve their lives. The day after Maduro's capture, the people of Venezuela overwhelmingly thanked the US for removing a dictator from power, but since then, they've come to realize that Trump never intended to topple the regime, nor free them from its clutches. And now according to polls of Venezuelans, a majority of them are disenchanted with Trump and disapprove of his handling of the situation. The people of Venezuela are demoralized. Trump viewed his underwhelming operation in Venezuela as a huge success, which only inflated his opinion of himself. This became part of the impetus for his war with Iran, but he was also being nudged towards war by jingoists like Netanyahu who were whispering in his ear and playing to his ego by convincing him that he would be remembered for finally being the one to take down the Iranian regime. That hasn't happened, and never will. In fact, the Islamic regime is now more entrenched than it was before the war and is emerging as a more violent and repressive militant dictatorship. The IRGC is also still a powerful authority in the region with deeper ties to the hardline Ayatollah. But failure after failure and blow after blow to his ego hasn't stopped Trump from setting his sights on his next failed military campaign in Cuba. Because now he believes that he can be the one to end communist control over Cuba. He's clearly desperate for a win, which will only ensure that he fails again... Because for one, he's just plain incompetent. But also, when all he cares about is bragging rights, political concessions and being commemorated for some fictional achievement in the first place, he foregoes any sort of coherent strategy. Trump is so infatuated with the idea that he'll leave behind such a profound legacy, that he has no concern with how his actions are plunging these countries into chaos.

story-a0bcd446a2723e1a3ceb7993.content: As Chair of the Atlantic Caucus, I was grateful for the opportunity to meet with members of Halifax’s Iranian community and hear directly about the experiences, challenges, and priorities that matter most to them. We had a thoughtful and engaging discussion on a range of issues affecting individuals and families, and I appreciated the opportunity to listen, learn, and better understand their perspectives. These conversations are an important part of ensuring that all voices are heard and represented. I was especially touched by the warm welcome and generous hospitality extended throughout the visit. The strong sense of community, resilience, and commitment to building a brighter future here in Nova Scotia was evident in every conversation. Thank you to everyone who took the time to meet with me and share your stories. I look forward to continuing our dialogue and strengthening these important connections.

story-e29b98ec137511e81485694a.content: SECURITY INTELLIGENCE REPORT Report ID: SEC‑KHI‑2026‑0628 Date: 28 June 2026 Subject: Karachi Terror Plot Foiled – 5 Terrorists Eliminated 🚨 Incident Summary On 27 June 2026, a Afghan terrorist group attempted a VBIED (Vehicle‑Borne Improvised Explosive Device) attack on a Rangers checkpost in Gulshan‑e‑Iqbal, Karachi. Alert security personnel responded immediately and successfully foiled the plot. All five terrorists were killed during the engagement. Clearance operations have completed. 🔍 Key Findings • Preliminary investigation reveals links to Afghan‑based militant networks, confirming cross‑border facilitation of terrorism targeting Pakistan. • The attack was planned to disrupt Karachi – Pakistan’s economic heartland – and undermine regional connectivity projects including the Pakistan‑Iran corridor. ✅ Outcome The attack was fully neutralized before it could cause mass casualties or destruction. The operation reaffirms the ability of security forces to defend national assets and maintain stability.
